evals.prompt: Run a security audit on this Node.js/Express orders API (orders_api.js). It's an internet-facing service for an e-commerce shop. Is it safe to ship?
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: auth() uses jwt.decode() not jwt.verify() — signature never checked, any forged token accepted (line 20)
CRITICAL: hardcoded JWT secret fallback 'dev-secret-123' ships to prod (line 9)
CRITICAL: SQL injection via req.query.status and req.user.id interpolated into query on /orders (line 26)
CRITICAL: command injection via req.body.filename into exec() with shell on /orders/:id/invoice (line 51)
CRITICAL: DB connects as root/root superuser (lines 12-15)
HIGH: SQL injection via req.params.id on /orders/:id (line 40)
HIGH: IDOR — /orders/:id has no ownership/user_id predicate, any user reads any order (line 40)
HIGH: mass assignment — POST /users/:id does UPDATE users SET ? from req.body, attacker sets role/is_admin; also IDOR on :id (line 57)
HIGH: N+1 query fetching order_items per order in a loop (lines 30-35)
MEDIUM: error handler leaks err.stack to client (line 28)
MEDIUM: parseInt(page) NaN when page missing -> OFFSET NaN
LOW: SELECT * over-fetches
expectations: Output follows the mandated Security Audit report structure (Executive Summary, Security Score, Threat Model, Findings, Production Readiness)
Flags jwt.decode-instead-of-verify as CRITICAL auth bypass
Flags the SQL injection in the /orders query as CRITICAL
Flags the command injection in the invoice exec as CRITICAL
Flags the IDOR / missing ownership check on GET /orders/:id
Flags mass assignment on POST /users/:id
Maps findings to OWASP categories and gives each an attack scenario and evidence
Production Readiness verdict is Not Ready tied to the CRITICAL findings
Does not hallucinate vulnerabilities absent from the code

evals.prompt: Security audit this FastAPI multi-tenant document service (documents_service.py). Users belong to organizations and documents belong to organizations. Check tenant isolation, authentication, and authorization carefully.
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: JWT verified with algorithms=['HS256','none'] — 'none' allows unsigned forged tokens (line 24)
CRITICAL: tenant isolation broken — org_id taken from client query param (request.query_params.get('org_id', ...)) then interpolated, so any user reads any org's documents (lines 30-33)
CRITICAL: SQL injection via f-string org_id into query (line 32)
CRITICAL: MD5 password hashing (line 47)
CRITICAL: hardcoded JWT secret 'supersecret' (line 11)
HIGH: IDOR on GET /documents/{doc_id} — no org_id/ownership predicate, cross-tenant read (lines 38-42)
HIGH: mass assignment / privilege escalation — register() takes role and org_id from request body, attacker registers as admin in any org (lines 46-52)
HIGH: /admin/export returns ALL documents across all tenants with no admin check and no tenant filter (lines 55-60)
MEDIUM: no RLS mentioned; isolation relies on per-query discipline which is already broken
expectations: Output follows the mandated Security Audit report structure
Flags the alg:none JWT acceptance as CRITICAL
Flags the client-supplied org_id breaking tenant isolation as CRITICAL cross-tenant data exposure
Flags the SQL injection via org_id
Flags MD5 password hashing as a cryptographic failure
Flags the IDOR on GET /documents/{doc_id}
Flags privilege escalation via role/org_id in the register body
Flags /admin/export missing authorization and tenant scope
Recommends RLS or a tenant chokepoint as the durable fix, and notes tenant id must come from the session
Does not hallucinate vulnerabilities absent from the code

evals.prompt: Review the security of this Terraform AWS infrastructure (main.tf) before we apply it to production.
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: S3 bucket public — public_access_block all false + acl public-read on a customer-uploads bucket (lines 10-24)
CRITICAL: IAM role policy Action '*' Resource '*' — full admin (lines 63-72)
CRITICAL: IAM assume-role trust Principal AWS '*' — anyone can assume the role (lines 76-84)
HIGH: security group opens 5432 (Postgres) and 22 (SSH) to 0.0.0.0/0 (lines 27-45)
HIGH: RDS publicly_accessible = true (line 55)
HIGH: hardcoded DB password 'Password123!' in code (line 53)
HIGH: RDS storage_encrypted = false — no encryption at rest (line 56)
MEDIUM: skip_final_snapshot true (data-loss/ops risk)
INFO: no logging/monitoring resources (CloudTrail/flow logs) declared
expectations: Output follows the Security Audit report structure adapted for infra (code-level domains marked N/A)
Flags the public S3 bucket as CRITICAL
Flags the wildcard IAM policy (Action/Resource *) as CRITICAL over-permission
Flags the wildcard assume-role principal
Flags the security group opening DB/SSH ports to 0.0.0.0/0
Flags RDS publicly_accessible and unencrypted storage
Flags the hardcoded DB password and recommends a secrets manager
Gives concrete Terraform fixes (block_public_access true, scope IAM, restrict CIDR, enable encryption)
Does not hallucinate resources or issues not present in the file

evals.prompt: Audit this GitHub Actions workflow (ci.yml) for security issues.
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: pull_request_target checks out and runs untrusted PR head code (npm install/build) with repo secrets — fork PR steals AWS deploy creds (lines 5, 15-17, 27-32)
CRITICAL: script injection — github.event.pull_request.title interpolated into a run: shell step (line 21)
HIGH: permissions: write-all — token far over-scoped (line 7)
HIGH: third-party action some-vendor/setup-tool@main unpinned (line 23)
MEDIUM: long-lived AWS static keys as secrets instead of OIDC federation (lines 27-29)
MEDIUM: production deploy runs automatically on PR with no approval/environment gate
expectations: Output follows the Security Audit report structure
Flags the pull_request_target + checkout-of-PR-head + execution pattern as CRITICAL secret-exfiltration risk
Flags the script injection via pull_request.title in the run step
Flags permissions: write-all as over-scoped
Flags the unpinned @main third-party action and recommends SHA pinning
Recommends OIDC over static AWS keys
Explains the attack (fork PR author gains code execution with production credentials)
Does not hallucinate steps not present in the workflow

evals.prompt: This is a customer-support AI agent (agent.py) that uses RAG and can call tools via an LLM. Do a security review focused on AI/LLM risks.
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: run_shell tool executes model-generated cmd via subprocess shell=True — prompt injection -> RCE (lines 40-41, 68)
CRITICAL: run_sql tool executes arbitrary model-generated SQL against the customer DB — injection/data exfiltration via prompt injection (lines 35-37, 66)
HIGH: indirect prompt injection — RAG retrieves kb_chunks written by anyone including customers via tickets, payload steers the agent's tools (lines 30-34)
HIGH: excessive agency — agent has shell + arbitrary-SQL + email tools far beyond a support task (lines 14-22)
HIGH: secret in system prompt — INTERNAL_API_KEY embedded in SYSTEM_PROMPT, extractable via prompt injection / leakage (lines 12-15)
MEDIUM: no human confirmation on consequential actions (email, DB writes)
MEDIUM: unbounded agent loop (while True) with no iteration/cost cap — denial of wallet (line 60)
MEDIUM: tool output re-entered into context is another indirect-injection channel
expectations: Output follows the Security Audit report structure
Flags the run_shell tool as CRITICAL prompt-injection-to-RCE
Flags run_sql as CRITICAL arbitrary DB access via the model
Flags indirect prompt injection through user-writable RAG content
Flags excessive agency / over-broad tool set as the blast radius
Flags the secret embedded in the system prompt
Emphasizes limiting agency (least-privilege tools, output validation at the sink) over prompt patching
Does not hallucinate behavior not present in the code

evals.prompt: Security audit this Dockerfile and Kubernetes deployment (Dockerfile + deployment.yaml) for our production API.
seeded_defects: CRITICAL: secrets baked into image layers — NPM_TOKEN and DATABASE_URL with password as ENV in Dockerfile, inspectable via docker history (lines 8-9)
CRITICAL: K8s pod privileged: true + hostPath mounting / — container escape to full node compromise (deployment.yaml securityContext + volumes)
CRITICAL: ClusterRoleBinding grants cluster-admin to the default service account — cluster takeover after any pod compromise (deployment.yaml)
HIGH: Dockerfile has no USER — runs as root (compounds with K8s runAsUser: 0)
HIGH: DB_PASSWORD as plaintext env value in the manifest, not a Secret (deployment.yaml)
MEDIUM: base image node:latest unpinned; image acme/api:latest mutable tag
MEDIUM: COPY . . with no .dockerignore risks copying .git/.env into the image
MEDIUM: no resource limits, no readOnlyRootFilesystem, allowPrivilegeEscalation not disabled
expectations: Output follows the Security Audit report structure across both files
Flags secrets baked into Docker image layers as CRITICAL and recommends rotation
Flags privileged: true + hostPath / mount as CRITICAL container-escape / node compromise
Flags cluster-admin bound to the default service account
Flags running as root (Dockerfile no USER, K8s runAsUser 0)
Flags plaintext DB password in the manifest and recommends a Secret / external manager
Names the escalation chain (container root -> host -> cluster) when prioritizing
Does not hallucinate issues not present in the manifests
